claim: Eval Bench unit and CLI behavior pass in the current local checkout.
expected: The unittest suite exits 0 and covers init, run, inspect, demo, pass, and fail behavior.
limits: This claim covers the recorded local test command only.

claim: Eval Bench can create a complete first-use demo project.
expected: The demo command writes eval cases, eval results, an eval report, command evidence, and proof cases.
limits: This claim covers the included starter demo path, not every possible user command.

claim: Eval Bench can inspect its generated eval results.
expected: The inspect command exits 0 and reports the system class, total cases, passed cases, and failed cases.
limits: This claim covers inspection of Eval Bench's own JSON output.

claim: Eval Bench can produce an installable Python wheel.
expected: The wheel build exits 0 and produces unit27_eval_bench-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl.
limits: This claim covers local artifact creation, not PyPI distribution.

claim: The built wheel contains the expected modules and console entry point.
expected: The wheel verifier exits 0 after checking core modules and eval-bench console script metadata.
limits: This claim covers package contents, not runtime installation in every environment.

claim: The public README stays inside the Eval Bench boundary.
expected: Boundary Engine scan exits 0 against the current README and Proof Ledger evidence.
limits: This claim covers the README, not every future public surface.
